title: Vector3D Object
description: Transient 3D vector. This object is a wrapper over 3D vector data and is used as way to pass vector data in and out of the API and as a convenience when operating on vector data. They are created statically using the create method of the Vector3D class.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Adds a vector to this vector.

methods.name: angleTo
methods.description: Determines the angle between this vector and the specified vector.

methods.name: asArray
methods.description: Returns the vector coordinates as an array [x, y, z].

methods.name: asPoint
methods.description: Returns a new point with the same coordinate values as this vector.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates a copy of this vector.

methods.name: create
methods.description: Creates a 3D vector object. This object is created statically using the Vector3D.create method.

methods.name: crossProduct
methods.description: Returns the cross product between this vector and the specified vector.

methods.name: dotProduct
methods.description: Returns the dot product between this vector and the specified vector.

methods.name: isEqualTo
methods.description: Determines if this vector is equal to the specified vector.

methods.name: isParallelTo
methods.description: Determines if the input vector is parallel with this vector.

methods.name: isPerpendicularTo
methods.description: Determines if the input vector is perpendicular to this vector.

methods.name: normalize
methods.description: Makes this vector of unit length. This vector should not be zero length.

methods.name: scaleBy
methods.description: Scale this vector by the specified product.

methods.name: setWithArray
methods.description: Reset this vector with the coordinate values in an array [x, y, z].

methods.name: subtract
methods.description: Subtract a vector from this vector.

methods.name: transformBy
methods.description: Transform this vector by the specified matrix.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: length
properties.description: Get the length of this vector.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Create Setups From Hole Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates how to create a correctly oriented setup using Hole Recognition functionality.The Fusion Manufacturing Extension is required for Hole Recognition.The script starts by opening a sample model from the CAM Samples folder via its URN. The model comprises a 3 way coupling containing holes in various orientations and mounted on a fixture. A reference setup is created for the model using a simple stock mode and offsets. The Hole Recognition feature of the Fusion Manufacturing Extension creates 5 hole groups containing 11 holes between them. For each unique hole group vector captured, a new setup is created and its orientation transformed to match the vector.

add.title: Vector3D.add Method
add.description: Adds a vector to this vector.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to add to this vector.

angleTo.title: Vector3D.angleTo Method
angleTo.description: Determines the angle between this vector and the specified vector.
angleTo.return_description: The angle in radians between this vector and the specified vector.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to measure the angle to.

related_samples.name: Rendering Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates using the Rendering capabilities in the API. This starts a series of local renderings to generate a series of frames, where the camera is repositioned and a parameter is modified for each frame to create a dynamic animation. To use the sample, have a design open that contains a parameter named "Length". It can be a user or model parameter. The sample will modify this parameter from a value of 0.1 cm to 15 cm, but you can change these values by editing the values of the paramStartVal and paramEndVal variables on lines 90 and 91 of the sample. It expects a folder named "C:\Temp\RenderSample" to exist, and will fail if it doesn't. The rendered frames will be written to that folder.An example rendering is shown below where this file was used. The parameter is modifying a move feature which results in changing the shape of an extrusion. The parameter could be driving anything and you could modify the code to edit more than one parameter. The result of this sample is a folder containing all of the rendered frames. You can process these to create an animation. The sample animation was created using GIMP.

asArray.title: Vector3D.asArray Method
asArray.description: Returns the vector coordinates as an array [x, y, z].
asArray.return_description: Returns the array of vector coordinates [x, y, z].

asPoint.title: Vector3D.asPoint Method
asPoint.description: Returns a new point with the same coordinate values as this vector.

classType.title: Vector3D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Vector3D.copy Method
copy.description: Creates a copy of this vector.

create.title: Vector3D.create Method
create.description: Creates a 3D vector object. This object is created statically using the Vector3D.create method.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = adsk.core.Vector3D.create()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = adsk.core.Vector3D.create(x, y, z)

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The optional x value.   This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.0.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The optional y value.   This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.0.

parameters.name: z
parameters.description: The optional z value.   This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.0.

crossProduct.title: Vector3D.crossProduct Method
crossProduct.description: Returns the cross product between this vector and the specified vector.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to take the cross product to.

dotProduct.title: Vector3D.dotProduct Method
dotProduct.description: Returns the dot product between this vector and the specified vector.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to take the dot product to.

isEqualTo.title: Vector3D.isEqualTo Method
isEqualTo.description: Determines if this vector is equal to the specified vector.
isEqualTo.return_description: Returns true if the vectors are equal.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to test equality to.

isParallelTo.title: Vector3D.isParallelTo Method
isParallelTo.description: Determines if the input vector is parallel with this vector.
isParallelTo.return_description: Returns true if the vectors are parallel.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to test parallelism to.

isPerpendicularTo.title: Vector3D.isPerpendicularTo Method
isPerpendicularTo.description: Determines if the input vector is perpendicular to this vector.
isPerpendicularTo.return_description: Returns true if the vectors are perpendicular.

parameters.name: vector
parameters.description: The vector to test perpendicularity to.

normalize.title: Vector3D.normalize Method
normalize.description: Makes this vector of unit length. This vector should not be zero length.

objectType.title: Vector3D.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = vector3D_var.objectType

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = vector3D_var.x

# Set the value of the property.
vector3D_var.x = propertyValue

# Get the value of the property.

# Set the value of the property.

# Get the value of the property.

# Set the value of the property.
